B1 Cell Biology.question: State two sub-cellular structures found in an animal cell. [2 marks]
mark_scheme: Nucleus (1 mark)
Cell membrane (1 mark)

B1 Cell Biology.question: Describe the function of mitochondria. [2 marks]
mark_scheme: Site of aerobic respiration (1 mark)
Releases energy for the cell (1 mark)

B1 Cell Biology.question: Explain one difference between prokaryotic and eukaryotic cells. [2 marks]
mark_scheme: Prokaryotic cells do not have a nucleus (1 mark)
Their DNA is free in the cytoplasm / may have plasmids (1 mark)

B1 Cell Biology.question: State the equation used to calculate magnification. [1 mark]
mark_scheme: Magnification = image size ÷ real size (1 mark)

B1 Cell Biology.question: Describe diffusion. [2 marks]
mark_scheme: Net movement of particles from an area of high concentration to an area of low concentration (1 mark)
Down a concentration gradient (1 mark)

B1 Cell Biology.question: Explain what is meant by osmosis. [3 marks]
mark_scheme: Net movement of water molecules (1 mark)
Through a partially permeable membrane (1 mark)
From a dilute solution to a more concentrated solution (1 mark)

B1 Cell Biology.question: Explain why active transport is needed to absorb mineral ions by root hair cells. [3 marks]
mark_scheme: The concentration of mineral ions in the soil can be lower than in the root hair cell (1 mark)
Mineral ions move against the concentration gradient (1 mark)
Energy from respiration is required (1 mark)

B1 Cell Biology.question: Describe what happens during mitosis. [4 marks]
mark_scheme: The DNA is copied before division (1 mark)
One set of chromosomes is pulled to each end of the cell (1 mark)
The nucleus divides (1 mark)
Two genetically identical daughter cells are produced (1 mark)

B2 Organisation.question: State the role of enzymes in the body. [2 marks]
mark_scheme: Biological catalysts (1 mark)
Speed up chemical reactions (1 mark)

B2 Organisation.question: Describe how protease helps in digestion. [2 marks]
mark_scheme: Protease breaks down proteins (1 mark)
Into amino acids (1 mark)

B2 Organisation.question: Explain the role of bile in digestion. [3 marks]
mark_scheme: Bile neutralises stomach acid (1 mark)
Produces alkaline conditions in the small intestine (1 mark)
Emulsifies lipids / increases the surface area for lipase (1 mark)

B2 Organisation.question: Describe the function of the heart. [2 marks]
mark_scheme: Pumps blood (1 mark)
Around the body / to the lungs (1 mark)

B2 Organisation.question: Compare arteries and veins. [3 marks]
mark_scheme: Arteries carry blood away from the heart whereas veins carry blood towards the heart (1 mark)
Arteries have thicker muscular walls (1 mark)
Veins have valves / a larger lumen (1 mark)

B2 Organisation.question: Explain how xylem tissue is adapted to its function. [3 marks]
mark_scheme: Xylem transports water and mineral ions (1 mark)
Cells are dead and hollow / form a continuous tube (1 mark)
Walls are strengthened with lignin (1 mark)

B2 Organisation.question: Describe transpiration. [3 marks]
mark_scheme: Water evaporates from the surface of mesophyll cells (1 mark)
Water vapour diffuses out through stomata (1 mark)
More water is pulled up through the xylem (1 mark)

B2 Organisation.question: Describe translocation in plants. [4 marks]
mark_scheme: Movement of dissolved sugars (1 mark)
In the phloem (1 mark)
From leaves to areas where needed or stored (1 mark)
Can occur in both directions (1 mark)

B3 Infection and Response.question: State what is meant by a pathogen. [1 mark]
mark_scheme: A microorganism that causes disease (1 mark)

B3 Infection and Response.question: Name two types of pathogen. [2 marks]
mark_scheme: Any two from bacteria / viruses / fungi / protists (1 mark)

B3 Infection and Response.question: Explain how bacterial diseases can make people ill. [2 marks]
mark_scheme: Bacteria reproduce rapidly inside the body (1 mark)
Some release toxins that damage tissues / make people feel ill (1 mark)

B3 Infection and Response.question: Explain how viruses cause disease. [2 marks]
mark_scheme: Viruses enter living cells (1 mark)
They reproduce inside cells and damage / burst them (1 mark)

B3 Infection and Response.question: Describe how white blood cells defend the body against pathogens. [3 marks]
mark_scheme: They can engulf pathogens by phagocytosis (1 mark)
They can produce antibodies (1 mark)
They can produce antitoxins (1 mark)

B3 Infection and Response.question: Explain how vaccination protects people from disease. [3 marks]
mark_scheme: A vaccine contains dead / inactive pathogens or their antigens (1 mark)
This stimulates white blood cells to produce antibodies / memory cells (1 mark)
The body responds more quickly if the real pathogen enters (1 mark)

B3 Infection and Response.question: Explain why antibiotics do not work against viruses. [2 marks]
mark_scheme: Antibiotics target bacterial processes / structures (1 mark)
Viruses reproduce inside body cells and do not have these targets (1 mark)

B3 Infection and Response.question: Describe how new medicines are tested before use. [4 marks]
mark_scheme: They are first tested in the laboratory (1 mark)
Then tested on tissues / live animals (1 mark)
Clinical trials are carried out on humans (1 mark)
They are checked for toxicity, efficacy and correct dosage (1 mark)

B4 Bioenergetics.question: Write the word equation for photosynthesis. [2 marks]
mark_scheme: Carbon dioxide + water (1 mark)
Glucose + oxygen (1 mark)

B4 Bioenergetics.question: State where photosynthesis takes place in a plant cell. [1 mark]
mark_scheme: Chloroplasts (1 mark)

B4 Bioenergetics.question: Explain why light intensity can affect the rate of photosynthesis. [2 marks]
mark_scheme: Light provides the energy for photosynthesis (1 mark)
At low light intensity light can be the limiting factor (1 mark)

B4 Bioenergetics.question: Describe one use of glucose made in photosynthesis. [2 marks]
mark_scheme: Used in respiration to release energy (1 mark)
Or converted to starch / cellulose / lipids / amino acids (1 mark)

B4 Bioenergetics.question: State the word equation for aerobic respiration. [2 marks]
mark_scheme: Glucose + oxygen (1 mark)
Carbon dioxide + water (1 mark)

B4 Bioenergetics.question: Explain one difference between aerobic and anaerobic respiration in animals. [2 marks]
mark_scheme: Aerobic respiration uses oxygen whereas anaerobic respiration does not (1 mark)
Anaerobic respiration releases less energy / produces lactic acid (1 mark)

B4 Bioenergetics.question: Describe how transpiration occurs. [3 marks]
mark_scheme: Water evaporates from mesophyll cells (1 mark)
Water vapour diffuses out through stomata (1 mark)
More water is drawn up through the xylem (1 mark)

B4 Bioenergetics.question: Explain how increasing temperature can affect the rate of transpiration. [4 marks]
mark_scheme: Water molecules gain kinetic energy (1 mark)
Evaporation from the leaf cells happens faster (1 mark)
Diffusion of water vapour out of the leaf increases (1 mark)
So the rate of transpiration increases (1 mark)

B5 Homeostasis and Response.question: State what is meant by homeostasis. [1 mark]
mark_scheme: The regulation of the internal conditions of a cell or organism to maintain optimum conditions for function (1 mark)

B5 Homeostasis and Response.question: State the three parts of a control system. [3 marks]
mark_scheme: Receptors (1 mark)
Coordination centre (1 mark)
Effectors (1 mark)

B5 Homeostasis and Response.question: Describe the role of receptors in the nervous system. [2 marks]
mark_scheme: Detect stimuli / changes in the environment (1 mark)
Send information as electrical impulses (1 mark)

B5 Homeostasis and Response.question: Explain what a reflex action is. [2 marks]
mark_scheme: A rapid automatic response to a stimulus (1 mark)
It helps protect the body from harm (1 mark)

B5 Homeostasis and Response.question: Describe how the eye focuses on a near object. [3 marks]
mark_scheme: Ciliary muscles contract (1 mark)
Suspensory ligaments slacken (1 mark)
Lens becomes thicker / more curved (1 mark)

B5 Homeostasis and Response.question: Explain how insulin lowers blood glucose concentration. [3 marks]
mark_scheme: Causes glucose to move from the blood into body cells (1 mark)
Causes glucose to be converted into glycogen (1 mark)
This happens in the liver / muscles (1 mark)

B5 Homeostasis and Response.question: Describe two ways the body responds when body temperature is too high. [2 marks]
mark_scheme: Sweating increases (1 mark)
Skin blood vessels dilate (1 mark)

B5 Homeostasis and Response.question: Explain the role of hormones in the menstrual cycle. [4 marks]
mark_scheme: FSH causes an egg to mature (1 mark)
Oestrogen rebuilds the uterus lining (1 mark)
LH triggers ovulation (1 mark)
Progesterone maintains the uterus lining (1 mark)

B6 Inheritance, Variation and Evolution.question: State where chromosomes are found in a cell. [1 mark]
mark_scheme: In the nucleus (1 mark)

B6 Inheritance, Variation and Evolution.question: State what a gene is. [1 mark]
mark_scheme: A short section of DNA that codes for a protein / characteristic (1 mark)

B6 Inheritance, Variation and Evolution.question: Describe what is meant by an allele. [1 mark]
mark_scheme: A different version of a gene (1 mark)

B6 Inheritance, Variation and Evolution.question: Explain the difference between genotype and phenotype. [2 marks]
mark_scheme: Genotype is the combination of alleles an organism has (1 mark)
Phenotype is the characteristics shown by the organism (1 mark)

B6 Inheritance, Variation and Evolution.question: State what is meant by a dominant allele. [1 mark]
mark_scheme: An allele expressed when only one copy is present (1 mark)

B6 Inheritance, Variation and Evolution.question: Describe natural selection. [3 marks]
mark_scheme: Individuals with advantageous characteristics are more likely to survive (1 mark)
They are more likely to reproduce (1 mark)
So advantageous alleles become more common in the population (1 mark)

B6 Inheritance, Variation and Evolution.question: Explain how antibiotic resistance can develop in bacteria. [4 marks]
mark_scheme: Mutations produce variation in the bacterial population (1 mark)
Some bacteria are resistant to the antibiotic (1 mark)
Non-resistant bacteria are killed (1 mark)
Resistant bacteria survive and reproduce / pass on the resistance allele (1 mark)

B6 Inheritance, Variation and Evolution.question: Describe one risk of selective breeding. [2 marks]
mark_scheme: Reduces genetic variation (1 mark)
The population may be more vulnerable to disease / inherited defects / environmental change (1 mark)

B7 Ecology.question: State what is meant by a population. [1 mark]
mark_scheme: All the organisms of one species in an area (1 mark)

B7 Ecology.question: State what is meant by a community. [1 mark]
mark_scheme: All the populations of different species in an area / ecosystem (1 mark)

B7 Ecology.question: Describe one abiotic factor that can affect the distribution of organisms. [2 marks]
mark_scheme: Any one from temperature / light intensity / moisture / soil pH / wind intensity / carbon dioxide level (1 mark)
Linked effect on the organism or its distribution (1 mark)

B7 Ecology.question: Explain what is meant by a trophic level. [2 marks]
mark_scheme: A feeding level in a food chain or food web (1 mark)
Shows the position of an organism in the transfer of biomass / energy (1 mark)

B7 Ecology.question: Describe the role of decomposers in an ecosystem. [3 marks]
mark_scheme: Break down dead plants / animals and waste materials (1 mark)
Release mineral ions back into the soil (1 mark)
Recycle materials through the ecosystem (1 mark)

B7 Ecology.question: Explain why quadrats are used in ecological sampling. [2 marks]
mark_scheme: Used to sample the distribution / abundance of organisms in an area (1 mark)
Useful when it is not practical to count every organism (1 mark)

B7 Ecology.question: Describe how a transect can be used to investigate distribution. [3 marks]
mark_scheme: A line is laid across the area (1 mark)
Quadrats are placed at regular intervals along the line (1 mark)
This shows how distribution changes across the habitat (1 mark)

B7 Ecology.question: Explain why maintaining biodiversity is important. [4 marks]
mark_scheme: Maintains stable ecosystems (1 mark)
Species may be useful in the future for food / medicine (1 mark)
Reduces the risk of extinction (1 mark)
Helps preserve habitats and genetic variation for future generations (1 mark)
